description: Public acceptance of the rVSV-ZEBOV vaccination program in the Democratic Republic of Congo during the Kivu Ebola outbreak. The mission, led by the WHO and the Ministry of Health, faced severe resistance from local communities, rumors, and conflict-driven mistrust.

key_events.description: WHO launches ring vaccination strategy in Beni and Butembo.
Militia attacks on health clinics lead to temporary suspension of teams.
Community engagement initiatives launched with local religious leaders.
Final assessment shows vaccine efficacy but persistent localized refusal.

calibration_notes: Ideal for modeling cultural resistance and the influence of non-state actors in undermining public health mandates.
